test_description: For each masked map, compute pseudo-a_lm up to lmax=31 and, for each multipole l, form the 3x3 angular-momentum power tensor A_ij(l) proportional to sum_{m m'} a_lm^* (J_i J_j)_{m m'} a_lm'. Take the principal eigenvector n_l of A_ij(l) as the multipole axis, with sign ignored. Separately form orientation matrices for odd and even multipoles: M_odd = mean(n_l n_l^T) over l=3,5,...,31 and M_even = mean(n_l n_l^T) over l=2,4,...,30. Let C_odd and C_even be the largest eigenvalues of these two matrices. The single anomaly statistic is S = C_odd - C_even, where positive values indicate stronger mutual alignment among odd-parity low-l multipole axes than among even-parity axes. Compute S for Planck SMICA and for each of the 1000 LCDM simulations using the identical mask and pipeline, then assess the Planck rank empirically.
test_hypothesis: Planck has an unusually large positive parity-split axis concentration contrast, i.e. odd low-l multipole axes are more mutually aligned than even low-l multipole axes, giving S = C_odd - C_even higher than expected under LCDM.
justificaton: The statistic is explicitly signed so that positive S indicates stronger odd-parity axis concentration relative to even parity, and the code labels the tail of interest as the upper tail with larger positive S representing the anomaly.

custom_summary.test_description: Low-l Parity-Split Axis Concentration Contrast: compute pseudo-a_lm through lmax=31 on the masked, zero-filled map; form each multipole's angular-momentum power tensor; extract sign-free principal axes; and return S = C_odd - C_even where C_parity is the largest eigenvalue of the mean n_l n_l^T over odd/even low-l multipoles.
custom_summary.statistic: S = C_odd - C_even, C_parity = largest eigenvalue of mean_l n_l n_l^T for angular-momentum principal axes
custom_summary.tail_of_interest: upper tail: larger positive S means stronger mutual alignment among odd-parity low-l axes relative to even-parity axes
custom_summary.masked_pixel_handling: non-finite pixels are treated as masked and zero-filled before healpy.map2alm; NaNs are never passed to healpy
custom_summary.axis_sign_treatment: axis signs are ignored via outer products n_l n_l^T

plot_spec.title: Low-l parity-split axis concentration contrast
plot_spec.caption: LCDM simulation distribution of S. Positive values indicate stronger mutual alignment among odd multipole axes (ell=3,5,...,31) than among even axes (ell=2,4,...,30); Planck is processed with the identical mask and pseudo-a_lm pipeline.

custom_summary.planck_contrast_direction: negative: Planck even-parity axes are more concentrated than Planck odd-parity axes by this statistic
custom_summary.interpretation: Planck is not in either 5% tail of the empirical LCDM distribution for this odd-minus-even low-l axis concentration contrast.

test_summary.Description: Uses masked Planck SMICA pseudo-a_lm to lmax=31, derives angular-momentum power-tensor axes for each multipole, compares odd- and even-parity axis concentration via S = C_odd - C_even.
test_summary.Results: Planck gives S = -0.0701, while LCDM simulations have mean -0.00131 and std 0.0852. For the pre-specified upper-tail hypothesis, p = 0.788 and z = -0.80; Planck is at about the 21st percentile, not in either 5% tail.
test_summary.Interpretation: The measured contrast is negative, indicating stronger even- than odd-parity axis concentration by this statistic. This is opposite to the hypothesised odd-axis excess and fully consistent with the simulation ensemble.
test_summary.Comparison to literature: Parity-split low-l directional alignments are related to prior work using power/alignment tensors; Aluri, Ralston & Weltman (2017, https://arxiv.org/abs/1703.07070) reported odd-multipole alignment evidence at roughly ≥2σ and no unusual even alignments, while Planck 2018 isotropy analyses (https://arxiv.org/abs/1906.02552) discuss persistent large-angle anomalies. This specific odd-minus-even concentration contrast does not reproduce an anomaly.
